title: CTest for the --emit-object + libeshkol-static.a manual-link AOT path (getenv + capability)
status_note: MERGED #88 / 1c876486241066ad4d1fdaf3bfd26ce8a41908eb. It adds static_link_getenv_capability_test: --emit-object compiles getenv allow/deny probes, manually links each with stdlib.o + libeshkol-static.a + configured host link args, verifies getenv reads a set var, and verifies an empty capability policy denies getenv. Local verification: cmake configure, eshkol-run/eshkol-static/stdlib build, focused CTest, direct harness, and ICC readiness ready/100. CI was green before merge; the inherited standalone VM-suite log gap was separately fixed by #90 / c032cefde047187248650b7ec48f79c5fce0af6b.
goal: Only eshkol-run -O2 AOT is covered; the --emit-object + manual c++ link against libeshkol-static.a path (used by downstream CMake consumers like bin/noesis) is untested. Add a CTest that: (1) compiles a getenv probe via --emit-object, links it against stdlib.o + libeshkol-static.a + required frameworks, runs it with a set var, asserts it reads the value; (2) asserts that an active capability policy without env-read correctly denies (intentional behavior, guards against silent regressions both ways).
acceptance: CTest links via the static-lib path and asserts getenv reads a set var
CTest asserts capability-gated denial is intentional
campaign_payoff: Covers the downstream static-link path that currently diverges from eshkol-run; catches getenv/capability regressions consumers hit.
